groups.text: It is currently 18 degrees and partly cloudy in Berlin-Tempelhof.
With the morning weather set, here is the news.

In the world of AI and robotics, NVIDIA is pushing the boundaries of physical intelligence. The company has unveiled new capabilities for its robots, focusing on a shift toward 'general-purpose' intelligence. By leveraging large-scale foundation models, NVIDIA aims to enable robots to learn complex tasks from observation and simulation, reducing the need for manual programming and allowing for faster adaptation to new environments.

Meanwhile, the geopolitical landscape remains volatile. In the Middle East, tensions persist as various actors navigate a complex security environment. Efforts to maintain stability are often hampered by conflicting interests and the risk of escalation, highlighting the ongoing challenge of achieving a lasting peace in the region.

Turning to the economy, central banks are closely monitoring inflation and employment data to determine the timing of potential interest rate cuts. Market analysts suggest that while some progress has been made, the path to price stability remains uncertain, necessitating a cautious approach to monetary policy in the coming months.
After the headlines, back to the weather.
This afternoon will be cloudy with rain chances up to 30 percent and temperatures between 22 and 23 degrees.
And from the weather, a quick look at the calendar.

On your calendar today: personal calendar: all day: Department Retro, at 10:00, Optikrunde, at 10:30, Research weekly at Gauss, at 11:00, MxM Spotlight, and at 15:00, Corsnero OffGPT, Gruppenmeeting Gregor, and Research Seminar.
And that's it for your personal morning broadcast.
